name: Coinbase Advanced Trade API
what_it_does: REST and WebSocket API for executing cryptocurrency trades, streaming real-time prices, and managing portfolios on Coinbase's regulated US exchange.
use_cases: Agents executing cryptocurrency buy/sell orders on a regulated US exchange
Portfolio monitoring agents tracking balances and open positions
Real-time price streaming via WebSocket for trading signal agents
Automated DCA (dollar-cost averaging) bots placing recurring market orders
Tax reporting agents pulling historical trade history and fills
not_for: Non-US users who need broader market access (many international markets restricted)
Users who need access to altcoins not listed on Coinbase (limited asset selection vs Binance)
Purely data/analytics use cases — market data APIs are cheaper
best_when: An agent needs to execute real cryptocurrency trades on a regulated, reputable US exchange with strong compliance posture and decent liquidity for major assets.
avoid_when: You need access to a wide range of altcoins, are outside the US and face regulatory restrictions, or need the lowest possible trading fees (Binance is cheaper).

auth.notes: Coinbase Developer Platform (CDP) API keys use Ed25519 key pairs with JWT signing. Legacy HMAC-based keys still supported but CDP keys are the modern approach. Scopes control trading vs read-only access. Requires a Coinbase account with identity verification (KYC).

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access is free; trading fees apply per transaction
pricing.paid_tiers: Trading fees: 0.05%-0.60% per trade depending on volume tier; market makers get lower rates; no subscription fee for API access
pricing.notes: No monthly API subscription cost — you pay trading fees on actual transactions. Fee structure is competitive for retail but more expensive than Binance at lower volumes. Must pass KYC identity verification to trade.

performance.rate_limits: 10 requests/second per endpoint; WebSocket supports multiple subscriptions per connection

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST responses include preview_failure_reason and error fields. WebSocket order updates carry status and reject_reason fields. HTTP status codes are semantically correct. Adequate for agent error handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order creation supports client_order_id for idempotency — submitting the same client_order_id twice returns the original order rather than creating a duplicate. Agents must generate and track client_order_id to safely retry failed requests.
known_agent_gotchas: Advanced Trade API replaced the old Coinbase Pro API — old Pro SDK is deprecated and will stop working; agents built on it must migrate
KYC identity verification is required before any trading API calls succeed — agents cannot be deployed for new users without manual onboarding
CDP API keys use JWT with Ed25519 — key generation is more complex than simple API key issuance and requires careful secret storage
US-only regulatory restrictions may block certain asset pairs or user types depending on state
Order fills are asynchronous — agents must poll or use WebSocket to confirm fill status, not just order submission success
Rate limits are per-endpoint (10 req/sec) — agents hitting multiple endpoints simultaneously can still be throttled

security_score_components.security_notes: Ed25519 JWT-based auth is strong. Scoped API keys allow read-only vs trading permissions. Coinbase is a regulated entity with strong compliance posture. KYC requirement adds identity assurance.
